title: [phase:critique] [family:notebooklm-auth] [provider_hint:codex] Review likely causes and fixes for `nlm auth` failing with `browser auth failed: failed to load page: websocket url timeout reached` on macOS, using only local workspace/system evidence. Focus on actionable next attempts, not generic ad...
summary_text: **Findings (local evidence only)** 1. `nlm` is failing at Chrome DevTools websocket bootstrap, not NotebookLM auth logic. Evidence: `nlm` wraps the error at [auth.go:230](/Users/masayuki/go/pkg/mod/github.com/tmc/nlm@v0.0.0-20260306170916-7c12ab8a839d/cmd/nlm/auth.go:230), and `chromedp` emits `websocket url timeout reached` when it cannot read `DevTools listening on ...` in time at [allocate.go:248](/Users/masayuki/go/pkg/mod/github.com/chromedp/chromedp@v0.11.2/allocate.go:248) and [allocate.go:286](/Users/masayuki/go/pkg/mod/github.com/chromedp/chromedp@v0.11.2/allocate.go:286). 2. This host currently does not expose a DevTools port even when Chrome is launched directly with `--remote-debugging-port`. Evidence: direct probes with Chrome on ports `9333/9555/9666/9777` all returned connection refused; logs show no DevTools line and end with `Terminating current process after 15 seconds with no connection` at [chrome-debug-wait.log:26](/Users/masayuki/Dev/tmp/chrome-debug-wait.log:26). 3. Profile-specific auth is not the issue; all profiles fail with the same websocket timeout. Evidence: `nlm auth login -d -all` failed on `Profile 3 [Chrome]`, `Default [Chrome Canary]`, and `Profile 1 [Chrome]` with identical timeout. 4. `NLM_USE_ORIGINAL_PROFILE=1` only affects the `-all` path; single-profile mode ignores it. Evidence: env var is read in the multi-profile path at [auth.go:138](/Users/masayuki/go/pkg/mod/github.com/tmc/nlm@v0.0.0-20260306170916-7c12ab8a839d/internal/auth/auth.go:138), but single-profile path always copies to temp dir at [auth.go:663](/Users/masayuki/go/pkg/mod/github.com/tmc/nlm@v0.0.0-20260306170916-7c12ab8a839d/internal/auth/auth.go:663). 5. With `NLM_USE_ORIGINAL_PROFILE=1 -all`, failure changes to Chrome singleton lock (`既存のブラウザ セッションで開いています。`), indicating existing browser-session interference is real. **Most actionable next attempts** 1. Fully quit Chrome and Canary (all windows/background processes), then retry: ```bash /Users/masayuki/bin/nlm auth login -d -all ``` 2. If you must test original profile mode (to avoid temp-profile copy), use only: ```bash NLM_USE_ORIGINAL_PROFILE=1 /Users/masayuki/bin/nlm auth login -d -all ``` and only after all Chrome/Canary processes are gone. 3. If websocket attach still fails, bypass browser automation entirely using `nlm`’s stdin parser (it accepts curl text and extracts cookies + `at=` token) at [auth.go:236](/Users/masayuki/go/pkg/mod/github.com/tmc/nlm@v0.0.0-20260306170916-7c12ab8a839d/cmd/nlm/auth.go:236): ```bash cat /path/to/copied-curl.txt | /Users/masayuki/bin/nlm auth ``` 4. If you patch `nlm`, highest-value fixes: - Add `chromedp.WSURLReadTimeout(...)` in auth allocator options (currently not set in [auth.go:679](/Users/masayuki/go/pkg/mod/github.com/tmc/nlm@v0.0.0-20260306170916-7c12ab8a839d/internal/auth/auth.go:679)). - Capture allocator stdout/stderr on timeout for diagnostics. - Make single-profile path honor `NLM_USE_ORIGINAL_PROFILE`. **Risk / test gaps** - No auth startup tests for macOS Chrome attach/singleton behavior; only refresh helpers are tested in [refresh_test.go:7](/Users/masayuki/go/pkg/mod/github.com/tmc/nlm@v0.0.0-20260306170916-7c12ab8a839d/internal/auth/refresh_test.go:7). - This makes regressions in DevTools bootstrap and profile-mode branching likely. **Lane evidence** - Lane A: static source/dependency trace (`nlm` auth code + `chromedp` timeout path). - Lane B: live runtime probes (`nlm auth -d/-all`, direct Chrome debug-port probes with logs). - Policy note: independent non-Codex reviewer lanes (Claude/GLM) were not invoked in this turn.
